author: Rogered
text:
Cover .balls/state-repo in gitignore + squash backstop

bl-ffb4's master_url mode materializes a balls-owned clone of the task hub at .balls/state-repo/. Like .balls/code-refs before bl-c4e2, it was created at the repo root but absent from both runtime-path defenses: store_init::ensure_main_gitignore (so a non-bare master_url repo carries an untracked clone a git add -A would commit) and review_safety::RUNTIME_PATHS (so the bl review squash backstop would not reject a state-repo tree that slipped past a stale .gitignore).

Add .balls/state-repo to both. The gitignore entry is unconditional, matching .balls/code-refs: state_repo::ensure materializes it from master_url, which is orthogonal to stealth mode. The backstop is mode-independent already — a path that never exists never matches.
